revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:桓武天皇, en:Emperor Kanmu, simple:Emperor Kanmu); add local discussion section + dated import note

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/90416617 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==今上天皇の発言について== 「天皇家は韓国人の血筋を引いている」、「皇室百済起源論」などの論理を大きく飛躍させる報道があった 実際には、そもそも征服王朝である百済において、扶余族たる百済王族と韓族たる民衆とでは言葉すら通じないほどの民族的差異があった 百済の民衆と現在の朝鮮半島に暮らす韓国人との間にすら血統的連続性はないこと、などの通説 　　これらの根拠は何でしょうか。民族的差異や血統等に関して、信頼のおける学術的な文献などありますでしょうか。通説というのは素人歴史家によるものも多いので、根拠となる文献等を明示しないまま百科事典に載せるべきではないでしょう。 「「天皇家が韓国人の血筋を引いている」という表現は明らかな誤謬である」というのも、言い過ぎでしょう。百済の血を引いていること自体は間違いないのです。皇室の起源は厳密には不明ですが、天皇家は、[[継体天皇]]他が、しばしば百済救援軍を送っており、少なくとも古代においては百済系の皇室と見る歴史研究者も多いのが事実です。 また、「いずれにしても、古代の話であるにもかからずこれが日韓双方で大きく話題にされたことは、天皇の発言が依然大きな影響力を持つことを物語る実例となった。」という部分は、数多いニュースの一つであり、それほど大きく話題になったわけでもなく、言い過ぎなので削除すべきでしょう。 以上、投稿ボタンを押したのだがシステム上の問題のためかノートに書き込みされなかったので、数日後に改めて書きました。失礼しました。--[[利用者:Ramakata|Ramakata]] 2007年5月12日 (土) 05:47 (UTC) 日本ではほとんど取り上げられなかったのは確かですが、韓国では三大紙で狂ったかのように取り上げられて、大統領談話にまで取り上げられたのですが（ｗ　もっとも狂ったように取り上げたのは韓国素人民間学者ばかりで、まともな学者は眉をひそめていましたがね。[[利用者:向日葵三十郎|向日葵三十郎]] 2007年5月12日 (土) 12:13 (UTC) : 「明らかな誤謬である」という箇所は独自考察ではないでしょうか。少なくとも出典を示さずにこの内容をWikipediaに掲載するのは問題があるでしょう。念のためテンプレートを貼っておきます。--[[利用者:Yoh-yoh|Yoh-yoh]] 2007年7月9日 (月) 21:52 (UTC) ::[[桓武天皇#今上天皇の発言]]の内容についてですが、[[#桓武天皇|桓武天皇]]の主題からかなり離れていると思います。そこで「[[ゆかり発言]]」というような新たな別の項目を作成して、記述を移動することを提案します。--[[利用者:敷島健一|敷島健一]] 2011年10月23日 (日) 04:09 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==在位年と西暦との対照表について== [[桓武天皇]]の「[[桓武天皇#在位年と西暦との対照表|在位年と西暦との対照表]]」は必要なのでしょうか？この時代は既に[[元号]]制度が採用されており、上代のように天皇の在位紀年が用いられることは無かったと思うのですが。-- [[利用者:元禄三百年生まれ|元禄三百年生まれ]] 2007年9月23日 (日) 11:40 (UTC) 桓武帝に関しては、朝鮮半島の争いを日本に展開するなど、まして東国を蝦夷と卑しめて自らの汚れた血統を隠すなど、あまり望まれた天皇ではなかった。桓武帝以後、日本は戦乱の世となったのも事実。今上天皇は源氏。歴史をつなぐために天皇家には桓武がおり朝鮮云々と言っているが、今上天皇は南朝系源氏であり、朝鮮人ではないことを念頭にいれてください。 :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 2008年6月3日 (火) 16:28の版の要約について == すいません、誤ってエンターキーを押してしまいました。執権北条氏と、[[稲毛重成]]のみの関連項目を追加した意図がわからない、もしくはなんらかの意図を感じることと、を削除した理由が不明であったため差し戻しました。--[[利用者:ごまふあざ|ごまふあざ]] 2008年6月3日 (火) 16:34 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1282055712 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|listas=Kammu|blp=no| {{WikiProject Biography|royalty-work-group=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|bio=y|history=y|royalty=y}} }} ==Kanmu versus Kammu== "Kanmu" or "Kammu", which one is right? [[User:Ryuch|Ryuch]] 07:28, 13 Oct 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? :There are many systems of romanization, that is, writing foreign words using the Roman alphabet. One system, which is conventional with the Japanese as well as foreign students learning Japanese, especially from a linguistic perspective, would spell it "Kanmu." Another system, which serves to communicate pronunciation to readers who don't speak Japanese, would write it "Kammu." So rather than a matter of right and wrong, it's a matter of purpose and readership. Personally, I believe "Kammu" better suits me, although "Kanmu" is more conventional among the Japanese and scholars. [[User:Fg2|Fg2]] 07:56, Oct 13, 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? ::Since this is an English wiki page, the romanization system used should be the one which best aids speakers of English. As this is generally the Hepburn System, I would suggest that "桓武"　should be rendered as "Kanmu" not "Kammu", as is the standard for that system. --[[User:Antarctic-adventurer|Antarctic-adventurer]] ([[User talk:Antarctic-adventurer|talk]]) 05:50, 4 December 2008 (UTC) :::Re: explanatory spelling note in first paragraph, see discussion thread at [[Talk:Emperor Temmu#Tenmu versus Temmu]]. --[[User:Tenmei|Tenmei]] ([[User talk:Tenmei|talk]]) 19:10, 28 January 2009 (UTC) ::::In [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/桓武天皇 Japanese] this emperor is called 桓武天皇（かんむてんのう) = Kanmu Tenno. So the most accurate writing is Kanmu which is also preferred by The [[Imperial Household Agency]]. The page name is Emperor Kanmu including on other language wiki pages. I recommend that the name be written as "Kanmu" in this wiki page. -[[User:Artanisen|Artanisen]] ([[User talk:Artanisen|talk]]) 15:10, 26 December 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Tangential links== An anonymous editor added several links in the "See also" section -- ''see'' [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Kammu&diff=308640380&oldid=306478117 here]. This Punjabi contributor made several similar edits: * 07:35, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) Emperor Kammu ‎ (→See also) * 07:31, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:26, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Fujiwara clan]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:22, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Kamakura]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:19, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Mimasaka Province]] ‎ (→References) * 07:17, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Hōnen]] ‎ (→Early life) In my view, the following are too much of a stretch in the context of [[Emperor Kammu]]: * [[Kamma]] * [[Kumo Xi]] * [[Saka]] [[Kamboja]] * [[Sakha Republic]] * [[Sakhalin]] * [[Indo-Aryan Migration]] * [[Turkic migration]] Whatever the intention, this needs more explanation and discussion. --[[User:Tenmei|Tenmei]] ([[User talk:Tenmei|talk]]) 12:47, 18 August 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == DOB == The first line of the article says he was born in 737, but the infobox says 735. Which is correct? [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] ([[User talk:LordAmeth|talk]]) 05:08, 16 January 2011 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Move discussion in progress== There is a move discussion in progress on [[Talk:Emperor Jimmu#Requested move|Talk:Emperor Jimmu]] which affects this page. Please participate on that page and not in this talk page section. Thank you. —[[User:RMCD bot|RMCD bot]] 10:43, 13 August 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Requested move==
:''The following discussion is an archived discussion of the proposal. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. No further edits should be made to this section. '' The result of the proposal was '''moved'''. --[[User:BDD|BDD]] ([[User talk:BDD|talk]]) 22:38, 22 October 2013 (UTC) * [[:Emperor Kammu]] → {{no redirect|Emperor Kanmu}} * [[:Emperor Kimmei]] → {{no redirect|Emperor Kinmei}} * [[:Emperor Mommu]] → {{no redirect|Emperor Monmu}} – Per [[Wikipedia:Manual of style (Japan-related articles)#Syllabic "n"|WP:MOSJ]], and for consistency with [[Emperor Ninmyō]], [[Empress Genmei]] and [[Emperor Tenmu]]. The [[Imperial Household Agency]] also prefers the proposed spellings.[https://www.google.co.jp/#filter=0&q=site%3Akunaicho.go.jp+%22Emperor+Kammu%22][https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=site:kunaicho.go.jp+%22Emperor+Kanmu%22&biw=1366&bih=667&filter=0][https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=site:kunaicho.go.jp+%22Emperor+Mommu%22#filter=0&q=site:kunaicho.go.jp+Emperor+Mommu][https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=site:kunaicho.go.jp+%22Emperor+Monmu%22] Every time I post one of these RMs I look through all the Google Books results and, honestly, I think I've demonstrated thoroughly enough that modern reliable print sources. If anyone else wants to take a shot this time, be my guest. But at this point I don't think it's necessary, because none of these moves have yet been opposed. [[User:Hijiri88|Hijiri 88]] ([[User talk:Hijiri88|聖]][[Special:Contributions/Hijiri88|やや]]) 13:48, 11 October 2013 (UTC) *'''Support''' – It's inconsistent to spell the sequence as "nm" in some and "mm" in others when they're the same. --[[User:Article editor|Article editor]] ([[User talk:Article editor|talk]]) 16:36, 18 October 2013 (UTC) :''The above discussion is preserved as an archive of the proposal. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on this talk page. No further edits should be made to this section.''
:Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == The false translation and edits lack of Neutrality == The [[User:Asieon]] reverted the edit of mine. [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Kanmu&diff=1281945873&oldid=1281945390 Difference]. This person cannot understand themselves. I wrote that this person hasn't proper competence of Japanese language. This person is the former indefinitely blocked user. At present, I do not find sock-pappetry of this user. The reason is not sock-pappetry, but false translation and the edits containing false and lack of Neutrality. I again point out and say that this editor has not proper, enough competence of Japanese language. Japanese language is very difficult language for non-Japanese users. Any languages are difficult for non-native speakers though. But Japanese language is especially very difficult since it is largely different from [[Indo-European languages]] and [[Sino-Tibetan languages]], and most languages in the world. Japanese language is isolated language, which has over 120,000,000 native speakers. [[Korean language]] is a bit similar to Japanese language, but still largely different. The words are totally different between Japanese and Korean, grammatical structures are as same. I should say additionally that even for native Japanese speakers, the [[old Japanese]] and ''[[Kanbun]]'' are very difficult, almost impossible to read and understand. The documents and references of the old history of Japan are written in Old Japanese and ''Kanbun''. ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', ''Kojiki'' and other documents of old Japan are very difficult to read and understand for this reason. Ones who want to edit the articles relating Japanese history and culture should have proper and sufficient understanding and competence of these (Knowledge of contemporary Japanese and old Japanese , and ''Kanbun'', in addition to deep and vast knowledge and understanding of Japanese culture, history and other various matters.) If ones do not have these knowledge and competence, do not edit (copy edit or other types of edits are possible though. But major edits are impossible for ones who have not knowledge of Japan and Japanese language). :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) === False translation === I explain the false translation by [[User:Asieon]] a bit in details here. The edit (added) by Asieon: : {{blockquote|{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓[[Yamato no Fuhito clan|和氏]]。諱[[Takano no Niigasa|新笠]]。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟[[Muryeong of Baekje|武寧王]]之子[[Prince Junda|純陀太子]]。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。[[Onjo of Baekje|其百濟]]遠祖[[Chumo of Goguryeo|都慕王]]者。[[Habaek|河伯]]之[[Lady Yuhwa|女]]感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}}
The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.|author=Emperor Kanmu|title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} The original ''Kanbun'' texts are as follows: :{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓和氏。諱新笠。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}} :(This text is suspicious. {{lang|ja|純陀太子}} is maybe false or wrong. In original text of ''Shoku Nihongi'', {{lang|ja|純陁太子}} is used. See [[:ja:純陀太子]]). :'''The meanings of the texts''' are as follows (a bit simplified): ::{{lang|ja| 00 明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。}} :::[Introduction] Fujiwara no ason Oguromaro says the Condolence/Lament (for the late Empress Dowager) on 14, January, in the year Shingai. ::{{lang|ja| 01 上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。 02 壬午。葬於大枝山陵。}} :::01] (The empress dowager was) given the Posthumous name ie., {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊}}. 02] (She was) buried at Ōe mount tomb, in the year of Jingo. ::{{lang|ja| 03a 皇太后姓和氏。03b 諱新笠。03c 贈正一位乙継之女也。03d 母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。}} :::03a] Surname of Empress Dowager is Yamato. 03b] Name is Niigasa. 03c] daughter of Ototsugu. 03d] mother is Ōe no ason Maimo. ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} :::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju) ::{{lang|ja| 05 皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。}} :::05] Empress has figure of virtues, of rich beauty. (It was) well-known (among people) from the early time. ::{{lang|ja| 06a 天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。06b 生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。}} :::06a] When Emperor (Kōnin) wasn't yet enthroned, (she) married with (him). 06b] Gave birth to Emperor (Kanmu), royal prince Sawara, royal princess Noto, at the Hōki period. ::{{lang|ja| 07 改姓爲高野朝臣。}} :::07] Surname was changed to Takano no ason. ::{{lang|ja| 08a 今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。08b 九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。}} :::08a] When Emperor (Kanmu) was enthroned, (she became) ''Bunin'' (Consort) Dowager. 08b] 9 years (later), higher title was given, ie. Empress Dowager. ::{{lang|ja| 09 其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。}} :::09] Her far ancestor of Kudara (Baekju), King Tobo, who was born when the daughter of Kahaku (River god) was inspired by Sun spirit. Empress Dowager is descendant of him. ::{{lang|ja| 10 因以奉諡焉。}} :::10] According to this (episode), the Posthumous name was decided. :'''The Structure of the Texts''': ::00] (Introduction:) ''Fujiwara no Oguromaro says the Condolence'' that: ::01] Posthumous name, 02] Burial place and time ::03a] (original) Surname, 03b] Name, 03c] Father, 03d] Mother ::04] Ancestor ::05] Figures ::06a] Marriage, 06b] Sons and daughter ::07] (present) Surname ::08a] (first) title as consort, 08b] (present) title as Consort ::09] The episode of ancestor ::10] ''Posthumous name was selected according to the above episode.'' :As shown above, it is very clear that all the entries in the Texts are selected and essential. They have the same importance. :The above contents and entries are clear for people who understand the ''Kanbun'' texts. But on the other hand, it is not clear at all, for those who do not read and understand Japanese language texts, Old Japanese and ''Kanbun'' texts. Even for the native Japanese speakers, the above texts are difficult to read and understand. The false English translation texts posted by [[User:Asieon]] (This is '''NOT mistranslation''', but '''False'''): :A] The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. B] Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. C] She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). D] Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. E] The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. F] The queen mother, [False-1]therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.| [False-2] author=Emperor Kanmu |title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} ::'''A]''' The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. ::'''B]''' Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. ::'''C]''' She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). ::'''D]''' Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. ::'''E]''' The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. ::'''F]''' The queen mother, [False-1] therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.| [False-2] author=Emperor Kanmu |title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} :'''Comparison with my translation:''' ::A == 03a and 03b : ("queen mother" is false. Possibly '''''output of machine translation''''') ::B =~ 05 : (not correct. possibly '''''machine translation''''') ::C =~ 06a and 06b : (lack of prince Sawara and princess Noto) ::D =~ 04 ::E ~- (09) : (but '''false translation''', Composition by the editor, '''Original Research and fantasy''') ::F -- '''no Correspondent Texts''' ::False-1 and False-2 -- '''False''' (There is no 1st personal pronoun in the original texts. The author of these texts is not Emperor Kanmu.) :There are so many and many problematic sentences that I cannot mention or point out one by one. Everything seem to be wrong, false, fantasy. :Please check my translation. As a matter of course, I cannot be perfect, still difficult to read old Japanese and ''Kanbun''. so possibly I made mistake in translation. But the stream of texts is maybe correct. :01 is the Posthumous name of Niigasa (mother of Emperor Kanmu), {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊 (あめ・たかしる・ひのこ・ひめ・の・みこと, Ame-takashiru-hinoko-hime-no-mikoto)}}. 09 is the reason why this Posthumous name was selected. The portion F of [[User:Asieon]] is as follows: :'''''The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.''''' In the original Texts, there are no words or sentences corresponding to this F statement. This is not mistranslation. But intentional false translation, or fantasy, propaganda, or other non-good faith edit. Who is "I" in this false translation? "False-2" says "Author: Emperor Kanmu". It means this "I" is Emperor Kanmu. Then the above statement is "''The queen mother, therefore I, Emperor Kanmu, am the direct descendant of them.''" But there is no such sentence in the original texts. The original texts say: ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} ::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). This is clearly written in the ''Shoku Nihongi''. But this is not considered as historical fact. The Japanese standard Encyclopedia cited in Kotobank do not show this as a historical fact. Father of Niigasa (grandfather of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|和乙継}} (Yamato no Ototsugu). Kotobank says: : {{lang|ja|和乙継 (?－?) 奈良時代の官吏。祖先は百済(くだら)(朝鮮)の人。}} : Yamato no Ototsugu (? - ?) [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%92%8C%E4%B9%99%E7%B6%99-1118051 Ototsugu in Kotobank]. :But no mention that he is descendant of the royal family of Baekje. Mother of Niigasa (grandmother of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|土師真妹}} (Haji no Maimo). There is no page of Haji no Maimo in Kotobank. The representative encyclopedia of Japan do not say that Niigasa is a descendant of the royal family of Baekju. But the statement in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' clearly writes Niigasa is a descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). It means this statement is not considered a historical fact. Sorry for long explanation. I do not want to write these things. (It is really hard work for me). 実際には、そもそも征服王朝である百済において、扶余族たる百済王族と韓族たる民衆とでは言葉すら通じないほどの民族的差異があった 百済の民衆と現在の朝鮮半島に暮らす韓国人との間にすら血統的連続性はないこと、などの通説 　　これらの根拠は何でしょうか。民族的差異や血統等に関して、信頼のおける学術的な文献などありますでしょうか。通説というのは素人歴史家によるものも多いので、根拠となる文献等を明示しないまま百科事典に載せるべきではないでしょう。 「「天皇家が韓国人の血筋を引いている」という表現は明らかな誤謬である」というのも、言い過ぎでしょう。百済の血を引いていること自体は間違いないのです。皇室の起源は厳密には不明ですが、天皇家は、[[継体天皇]]他が、しばしば百済救援軍を送っており、少なくとも古代においては百済系の皇室と見る歴史研究者も多いのが事実です。 また、「いずれにしても、古代の話であるにもかからずこれが日韓双方で大きく話題にされたことは、天皇の発言が依然大きな影響力を持つことを物語る実例となった。」という部分は、数多いニュースの一つであり、それほど大きく話題になったわけでもなく、言い過ぎなので削除すべきでしょう。 [[User:Ryuch|Ryuch]] 07:28, 13 Oct 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? :There are many systems of romanization, that is, writing foreign words using the Roman alphabet. One system, which is conventional with the Japanese as well as foreign students learning Japanese, especially from a linguistic perspective, would spell it "Kanmu." Another system, which serves to communicate pronunciation to readers who don't speak Japanese, would write it "Kammu." So rather than a matter of right and wrong, it's a matter of purpose and readership. Personally, I believe "Kammu" better suits me, although "Kanmu" is more conventional among the Japanese and scholars. [[User:Fg2|Fg2]] 07:56, Oct 13, 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? ::Since this is an English wiki page, the romanization system used should be the one which best aids speakers of English. The edit (added) by Asieon: : {{blockquote|{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓[[Yamato no Fuhito clan|和氏]]。諱[[Takano no Niigasa|新笠]]。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟[[Muryeong of Baekje|武寧王]]之子[[Prince Junda|純陀太子]]。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。[[Onjo of Baekje|其百濟]]遠祖[[Chumo of Goguryeo|都慕王]]者。[[Habaek|河伯]]之[[Lady Yuhwa|女]]感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}}
The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.|author=Emperor Kanmu|title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} The original ''Kanbun'' texts are as follows: :{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓和氏。諱新笠。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}} :(This text is suspicious. {{lang|ja|純陀太子}} is maybe false or wrong. In original text of ''Shoku Nihongi'', {{lang|ja|純陁太子}} is used. See [[:ja:純陀太子]]). :'''The meanings of the texts''' are as follows (a bit simplified): ::{{lang|ja| 00 明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。}} :::[Introduction] Fujiwara no ason Oguromaro says the Condolence/Lament (for the late Empress Dowager) on 14, January, in the year Shingai. ::{{lang|ja| 01 上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。 02 壬午。葬於大枝山陵。}} :::01] (The empress dowager was) given the Posthumous name ie., {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊}}. 02] (She was) buried at Ōe mount tomb, in the year of Jingo. ::{{lang|ja| 03a 皇太后姓和氏。03b 諱新笠。03c 贈正一位乙継之女也。03d 母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。}} :::03a] Surname of Empress Dowager is Yamato. 03b] Name is Niigasa. 03c] daughter of Ototsugu. 03d] mother is Ōe no ason Maimo. ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} :::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju) ::{{lang|ja| 05 皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。}} :::05] Empress has figure of virtues, of rich beauty. (It was) well-known (among people) from the early time. ::{{lang|ja| 06a 天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。06b 生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。}} :::06a] When Emperor (Kōnin) wasn't yet enthroned, (she) married with (him). 06b] Gave birth to Emperor (Kanmu), royal prince Sawara, royal princess Noto, at the Hōki period. ::{{lang|ja| 07 改姓爲高野朝臣。}} :::07] Surname was changed to Takano no ason. ::{{lang|ja| 08a 今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。08b 九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。}} :::08a] When Emperor (Kanmu) was enthroned, (she became) ''Bunin'' (Consort) Dowager. 08b] 9 years (later), higher title was given, ie. Empress Dowager. ::{{lang|ja| 09 其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。}} :::09] Her far ancestor of Kudara (Baekju), King Tobo, who was born when the daughter of Kahaku (River god) was inspired by Sun spirit. Empress Dowager is descendant of him. ::{{lang|ja| 10 因以奉諡焉。}} :::10] According to this (episode), the Posthumous name was decided. :'''The Structure of the Texts''': ::00] (Introduction:) ''Fujiwara no Oguromaro says the Condolence'' that: ::01] Posthumous name, 02] Burial place and time ::03a] (original) Surname, 03b] Name, 03c] Father, 03d] Mother ::04] Ancestor ::05] Figures ::06a] Marriage, 06b] Sons and daughter ::07] (present) Surname ::08a] (first) title as consort, 08b] (present) title as Consort ::09] The episode of ancestor ::10] ''Posthumous name was selected according to the above episode.'' :As shown above, it is very clear that all the entries in the Texts are selected and essential. They have the same importance. :The above contents and entries are clear for people who understand the ''Kanbun'' texts. But on the other hand, it is not clear at all, for those who do not read and understand Japanese language texts, Old Japanese and ''Kanbun'' texts. Even for the native Japanese speakers, the above texts are difficult to read and understand. The false English translation texts posted by [[User:Asieon]] (This is '''NOT mistranslation''', but '''False'''): :A] The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. B] Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. C] She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). D] Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. E] The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. F] The queen mother, [False-1]therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.| [False-2] author=Emperor Kanmu |title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} ::'''A]''' The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. ::'''B]''' Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. ::'''C]''' She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). ::'''D]''' Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. ::'''E]''' The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. ::'''F]''' The queen mother, [False-1] therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.| [False-2] author=Emperor Kanmu |title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} :'''Comparison with my translation:''' ::A == 03a and 03b : ("queen mother" is false. Possibly '''''output of machine translation''''') ::B =~ 05 : (not correct. possibly '''''machine translation''''') ::C =~ 06a and 06b : (lack of prince Sawara and princess Noto) ::D =~ 04 ::E ~- (09) : (but '''false translation''', Composition by the editor, '''Original Research and fantasy''') ::F -- '''no Correspondent Texts''' ::False-1 and False-2 -- '''False''' (There is no 1st personal pronoun in the original texts. The author of these texts is not Emperor Kanmu.) :There are so many and many problematic sentences that I cannot mention or point out one by one. Everything seem to be wrong, false, fantasy. :Please check my translation. As a matter of course, I cannot be perfect, still difficult to read old Japanese and ''Kanbun''. so possibly I made mistake in translation. But the stream of texts is maybe correct. :01 is the Posthumous name of Niigasa (mother of Emperor Kanmu), {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊 (あめ・たかしる・ひのこ・ひめ・の・みこと, Ame-takashiru-hinoko-hime-no-mikoto)}}. 09 is the reason why this Posthumous name was selected. The portion F of [[User:Asieon]] is as follows: :'''''The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.''''' In the original Texts, there are no words or sentences corresponding to this F statement. This is not mistranslation. But intentional false translation, or fantasy, propaganda, or other non-good faith edit. Who is "I" in this false translation? "False-2" says "Author: Emperor Kanmu". It means this "I" is Emperor Kanmu. Then the above statement is "''The queen mother, therefore I, Emperor Kanmu, am the direct descendant of them.''" But there is no such sentence in the original texts. The original texts say: ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} ::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). This is clearly written in the ''Shoku Nihongi''. But this is not considered as historical fact. The Japanese standard Encyclopedia cited in Kotobank do not show this as a historical fact. Father of Niigasa (grandfather of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|和乙継}} (Yamato no Ototsugu). Kotobank says: : {{lang|ja|和乙継 (?－?) 奈良時代の官吏。祖先は百済(くだら)(朝鮮)の人。}} : Yamato no Ototsugu (? - ?) An official in the Nara period. His ancestor is people of Kudara (Baekje), Chōsen (korea). [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%92%8C%E4%B9%99%E7%B6%99-1118051 Ototsugu in Kotobank]. :But no mention that he is descendant of the royal family of Baekje. Mother of Niigasa (grandmother of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|土師真妹}} (Haji no Maimo). There is no page of Haji no Maimo in Kotobank. The representative encyclopedia of Japan do not say that Niigasa is a descendant of the royal family of Baekju. But the statement in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' clearly writes Niigasa is a descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). It means this statement is not considered a historical fact. Sorry for long explanation. I do not want to write these things. (It is really hard work for me). I said the simple and short message that: the editor has not proper competence of Japanese language. I hope by this message, the editor (user Asieon) gets aware of their lack of competence. But not. The most people, who have not knowledge of Japanese language and history of Japan, possibly do not get aware of this false translation. To make clear of it, the long explanations are required. As to the [[User:Asieon]], they don't seem to understand the reason of their indefinite block. The block is essentially not due to sock-pappetry. It is due to their behaviors are against the basic guidelines of the Wikipedia. Reliable Sources, Not Original Research, Neutrality, Constructive edits etc. Posting the apparent false insisting is against the guidelines.-- [[User:Flora fon Esth|Flora fon Esth]] ([[User talk:Flora fon Esth|talk]]) 21:24, 23 March 2025 (UTC) :You are clearly not a native speaker of English, nor am I a native speaker of Japanese. However, it is absolutely unprofessional to put words into other people's mouths. You stated that the information was originally "'''Vandalism'''". I said it was not, because it does not constitute as one. You said in an invisible comment that your reasoning was due to "sockpuppetry", which again has been resolved. I even stated that you can add more information if needed. :"''This does not classify as vandalism. If information is needed to be changed, feel free to do so. Also, account was redeemed by an admin, therefore, sockpuppetry reasoning is now void.''" :That is all I said. [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Kanmu&diff=1281945873&oldid=1281945390 I even added your suggestion, crediting your name and your contribution.] Your accusation of my proficiency in Japanese has '''''NOTHING''''' to do with the actions I took previous to this informative, but misdirected comment. :''"The block is essentially not due to sock-pappetry. It is due to their behaviors are against the basic guidelines of the Wikipedia. Reliable Sources, Not Original Research, Neutrality, Constructive edits etc. Posting the apparent false insisting is against the guidelines."'' :This is factually not true. Please refrain from spreading false information just to make your arguments look better. Sockpuppetry and vandalism are completely different accusations. I don't understand why you are making this personal, but I will be the bigger person and refrain from commenting on it any further. [[User:Asieon|Asieon]] ([[User talk:Asieon|talk]]) 23:58, 23 March 2025 (UTC) :I see many problems in your translation as well. :01/05/06 - Are already mentioned. Though it is not word by word, the essence of the message is there. :04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。 :　04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju) :There is no King Bunei(?) of Kudara(?). That is not a name of a king nor a kingdom. 武寧王 (King Bunei) is [[Muryeong of Baekje]] and 百濟 (Kudara) is the kingdom of [[Baekje]]. :09 其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。 :: 09] Her far ancestor of Kudara (Baekju), King Tobo, who was born when the daughter of Kahaku (River god) was inspired by Sun spirit. Empress Dowager is descendant of him. :Again, King Tobo (都慕王) is [[Dongmyeong of Goguryeo]], Kahaku (河伯) is [[Habaek]], the daughter of Kahaku is [[Lady Yuhwa]] also known as "Habaeknyeo (河伯女)". It seems to me that your argument revolves around [[Literal translation|literal translations]] of the passages found in the Shoku Nihongi, where for convenience sake, I believe the overall meaning behind the statement is more important here. :Kammu is clearly stating or emphasizing his holy connections to his ancestors to honor his late mother '''AND''' her clan. Otherwise, there would be no reason to add this piece of information alluding to people who have nothing to do with the Japanese Imperial line. Not only does he encompass his immediate Japanese lineage, he also stretches into the kings of ancient [[Baekje]], the founder, and even the King of [[Goguryeo]], his mother from [[Buyeo]] and a river god. :[[Emperor Kanmu]] (Japan) → [[Takano no Niigasa]] (Japan) → [[Prince Junda]] (Japan) → [[Muryeong of Baekje]] (Baekje) → [[Onjo of Baekje]] (Baekje) → [[Dongmyeong of Goguryeo]] (Goguryeo) → [[Lady Yuhwa]] (Buyeo) → [[Habaek]] (God) :Again, I will concede the point that the translation isn't perfect, but that can be fixed. The only reason why the information was there in the first place was to indicate Kanmu's show of interest in his foreign bloodline. That fact is evident in his foreign/domestic policies revolving around other clans of Baekje origins PLUS his marriage to other Korea-originating clan women. This was just to preface why he was so open-minded. [[User:Asieon|Asieon]] ([[User talk:Asieon|talk]]) 00:20, 24 March 2025 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Simple English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/6346949 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{translated page|en|Emperor Kanmu|version=455480140}}

revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/90416617 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==今上天皇の発言について== 「天皇家は韓国人の血筋を引いている」、「皇室百済起源論」などの論理を大きく飛躍させる報道があった 実際には、そもそも征服王朝である百済において、扶余族たる百済王族と韓族たる民衆とでは言葉すら通じないほどの民族的差異があった 百済の民衆と現在の朝鮮半島に暮らす韓国人との間にすら血統的連続性はないこと、などの通説 　　これらの根拠は何でしょうか。民族的差異や血統等に関して、信頼のおける学術的な文献などありますでしょうか。通説というのは素人歴史家によるものも多いので、根拠となる文献等を明示しないまま百科事典に載せるべきではないでしょう。 「「天皇家が韓国人の血筋を引いている」という表現は明らかな誤謬である」というのも、言い過ぎでしょう。百済の血を引いていること自体は間違いないのです。皇室の起源は厳密には不明ですが、天皇家は、[[継体天皇]]他が、しばしば百済救援軍を送っており、少なくとも古代においては百済系の皇室と見る歴史研究者も多いのが事実です。 また、「いずれにしても、古代の話であるにもかからずこれが日韓双方で大きく話題にされたことは、天皇の発言が依然大きな影響力を持つことを物語る実例となった。」という部分は、数多いニュースの一つであり、それほど大きく話題になったわけでもなく、言い過ぎなので削除すべきでしょう。 以上、投稿ボタンを押したのだがシステム上の問題のためかノートに書き込みされなかったので、数日後に改めて書きました。失礼しました。--[[User:Ramakata|Ramakata]] 2007年5月12日 (土) 05:47 (UTC) 日本ではほとんど取り上げられなかったのは確かですが、韓国では三大紙で狂ったかのように取り上げられて、大統領談話にまで取り上げられたのですが（ｗ　もっとも狂ったように取り上げたのは韓国素人民間学者ばかりで、まともな学者は眉をひそめていましたがね。[[User:向日葵三十郎|向日葵三十郎]] 2007年5月12日 (土) 12:13 (UTC) : 「明らかな誤謬である」という箇所は独自考察ではないでしょうか。少なくとも出典を示さずにこの内容をWikipediaに掲載するのは問題があるでしょう。念のためテンプレートを貼っておきます。--[[User:Yoh-yoh|Yoh-yoh]] 2007年7月9日 (月) 21:52 (UTC) ::[[桓武天皇#今上天皇の発言]]の内容についてですが、[[#桓武天皇|桓武天皇]]の主題からかなり離れていると思います。そこで「[[ゆかり発言]]」というような新たな別の項目を作成して、記述を移動することを提案します。--[[User:敷島健一|敷島健一]] 2011年10月23日 (日) 04:09 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==在位年と西暦との対照表について== [[桓武天皇]]の「[[桓武天皇#在位年と西暦との対照表|在位年と西暦との対照表]]」は必要なのでしょうか？この時代は既に[[元号]]制度が採用されており、上代のように天皇の在位紀年が用いられることは無かったと思うのですが。-- [[User:元禄三百年生まれ|元禄三百年生まれ]] 2007年9月23日 (日) 11:40 (UTC) 桓武帝に関しては、朝鮮半島の争いを日本に展開するなど、まして東国を蝦夷と卑しめて自らの汚れた血統を隠すなど、あまり望まれた天皇ではなかった。桓武帝以後、日本は戦乱の世となったのも事実。今上天皇は源氏。歴史をつなぐために天皇家には桓武がおり朝鮮云々と言っているが、今上天皇は南朝系源氏であり、朝鮮人ではないことを念頭にいれてください。 :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == 2008年6月3日 (火) 16:28の版の要約について == すいません、誤ってエンターキーを押してしまいました。執権北条氏と、[[稲毛重成]]のみの関連項目を追加した意図がわからない、もしくはなんらかの意図を感じることと、を削除した理由が不明であったため差し戻しました。--[[User:ごまふあざ|ごまふあざ]] 2008年6月3日 (火) 16:34 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1282055712 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|listas=Kammu|blp=no| {{WikiProject Biography|royalty-work-group=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=high|bio=y|history=y|royalty=y}} }} ==Kanmu versus Kammu== "Kanmu" or "Kammu", which one is right? [[User:Ryuch|Ryuch]] 07:28, 13 Oct 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? :There are many systems of romanization, that is, writing foreign words using the Roman alphabet. One system, which is conventional with the Japanese as well as foreign students learning Japanese, especially from a linguistic perspective, would spell it "Kanmu." Another system, which serves to communicate pronunciation to readers who don't speak Japanese, would write it "Kammu." So rather than a matter of right and wrong, it's a matter of purpose and readership. Personally, I believe "Kammu" better suits me, although "Kanmu" is more conventional among the Japanese and scholars. [[User:Fg2|Fg2]] 07:56, Oct 13, 2004 -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:Emperor_Kammu&diff=next&oldid=22549693 diff]? ::Since this is an English wiki page, the romanization system used should be the one which best aids speakers of English. As this is generally the Hepburn System, I would suggest that "桓武"　should be rendered as "Kanmu" not "Kammu", as is the standard for that system. --[[User:Antarctic-adventurer|Antarctic-adventurer]] ([[User talk:Antarctic-adventurer|talk]]) 05:50, 4 December 2008 (UTC) :::Re: explanatory spelling note in first paragraph, see discussion thread at [[Talk:Emperor Temmu#Tenmu versus Temmu]]. --[[User:Tenmei|Tenmei]] ([[User talk:Tenmei|talk]]) 19:10, 28 January 2009 (UTC) ::::In [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/桓武天皇 Japanese] this emperor is called 桓武天皇（かんむてんのう) = Kanmu Tenno. So the most accurate writing is Kanmu which is also preferred by The [[Imperial Household Agency]]. The page name is Emperor Kanmu including on other language wiki pages. I recommend that the name be written as "Kanmu" in this wiki page. -[[User:Artanisen|Artanisen]] ([[User talk:Artanisen|talk]]) 15:10, 26 December 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Tangential links== An anonymous editor added several links in the "See also" section -- ''see'' [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Kammu&diff=308640380&oldid=306478117 here]. This Punjabi contributor made several similar edits: * 07:35, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) Emperor Kammu ‎ (→See also) * 07:31, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:26, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Fujiwara clan]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:22, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Kamakura]] ‎ (→See also) * 07:19, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Mimasaka Province]] ‎ (→References) * 07:17, 18 August 2009 (hist) (diff) [[Hōnen]] ‎ (→Early life) In my view, the following are too much of a stretch in the context of [[Emperor Kammu]]: * [[Kamma]] * [[Kumo Xi]] * [[Saka]] [[Kamboja]] * [[Sakha Republic]] * [[Sakhalin]] * [[Indo-Aryan Migration]] * [[Turkic migration]] Whatever the intention, this needs more explanation and discussion. --[[User:Tenmei|Tenmei]] ([[User talk:Tenmei|talk]]) 12:47, 18 August 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:18, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == DOB == The first line of the article says he was born in 737, but the infobox says 735. Which is correct? The edit (added) by Asieon: : {{blockquote|{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓[[Yamato no Fuhito clan|和氏]]。諱[[Takano no Niigasa|新笠]]。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟[[Muryeong of Baekje|武寧王]]之子[[Prince Junda|純陀太子]]。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。[[Onjo of Baekje|其百濟]]遠祖[[Chumo of Goguryeo|都慕王]]者。[[Habaek|河伯]]之[[Lady Yuhwa|女]]感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}}
The queen mother's clan (surname) was [[Yamato no Fuhito clan|Yamato]] and her name was [[Takano no Niigasa|Niigasa]]. Her majesty had astonishing looks that spoke of maturity that was quickly recognized. She was married to [[Emperor Kōnin]] before he was officially inaugurated when she had his son (Kammu/Yamabe). Her ancestor was the son of King [[Muryeong of Baekje]], [[Prince Junda]]. The [[Onjo of Baekje|king of Baekje]] is said to be the descendant of [[Chumo of Goguryeo|King Dongmyeong]] who hatched from an egg when his [[Lady Yuhwa|mother]], the daughter of [[Habaek]] was touched by sunlight. The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.|author=Emperor Kanmu|title=[[Shoku Nihongi]]|source=Enryaku, 9th year, January 15th}} The original ''Kanbun'' texts are as follows: :{{lang|ja|明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。壬午。葬於大枝山陵。皇太后姓和氏。諱新笠。贈正一位乙継之女也。母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。改姓爲高野朝臣。今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。因以奉諡焉。}} :(This text is suspicious. {{lang|ja|純陀太子}} is maybe false or wrong. In original text of ''Shoku Nihongi'', {{lang|ja|純陁太子}} is used. See [[:ja:純陀太子]]). :'''The meanings of the texts''' are as follows (a bit simplified): ::{{lang|ja| 00 明年正月十四日辛亥。中納言正三位藤原朝臣小黒麻呂率誄人奉誄。}} :::[Introduction] Fujiwara no ason Oguromaro says the Condolence/Lament (for the late Empress Dowager) on 14, January, in the year Shingai. ::{{lang|ja| 01 上諡曰天高知日之子姫尊。 02 壬午。葬於大枝山陵。}} :::01] (The empress dowager was) given the Posthumous name ie., {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊}}. 02] (She was) buried at Ōe mount tomb, in the year of Jingo. ::{{lang|ja| 03a 皇太后姓和氏。03b 諱新笠。03c 贈正一位乙継之女也。03d 母贈正一位大枝朝臣眞妹。}} :::03a] Surname of Empress Dowager is Yamato. 03b] Name is Niigasa. 03c] daughter of Ototsugu. 03d] mother is Ōe no ason Maimo. ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} :::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju) ::{{lang|ja| 05 皇后容徳淑茂。夙著聲譽。}} :::05] Empress has figure of virtues, of rich beauty. (It was) well-known (among people) from the early time. ::{{lang|ja| 06a 天宗高紹天皇龍潜之日。娉而納焉。06b 生今上。早良親王。能登内親王。寳龜年中。}} :::06a] When Emperor (Kōnin) wasn't yet enthroned, (she) married with (him). 06b] Gave birth to Emperor (Kanmu), royal prince Sawara, royal princess Noto, at the Hōki period. ::{{lang|ja| 07 改姓爲高野朝臣。}} :::07] Surname was changed to Takano no ason. ::{{lang|ja| 08a 今上即位。尊爲皇太夫人。08b 九年追上尊號。曰皇太后。}} :::08a] When Emperor (Kanmu) was enthroned, (she became) ''Bunin'' (Consort) Dowager. 08b] 9 years (later), higher title was given, ie. Empress Dowager. ::{{lang|ja| 09 其百濟遠祖都慕王者。河伯之女感日精而所生。皇太后即其後也。}} :::09] Her far ancestor of Kudara (Baekju), King Tobo, who was born when the daughter of Kahaku (River god) was inspired by Sun spirit. Empress Dowager is descendant of him. ::{{lang|ja| 10 因以奉諡焉。}} :::10] According to this (episode), the Posthumous name was decided. :'''The Structure of the Texts''': ::00] (Introduction:) ''Fujiwara no Oguromaro says the Condolence'' that: ::01] Posthumous name, 02] Burial place and time ::03a] (original) Surname, 03b] Name, 03c] Father, 03d] Mother ::04] Ancestor ::05] Figures ::06a] Marriage, 06b] Sons and daughter ::07] (present) Surname ::08a] (first) title as consort, 08b] (present) title as Consort ::09] The episode of ancestor ::10] ''Posthumous name was selected according to the above episode.'' :As shown above, it is very clear that all the entries in the Texts are selected and essential. They have the same importance. :The above contents and entries are clear for people who understand the ''Kanbun'' texts. But on the other hand, it is not clear at all, for those who do not read and understand Japanese language texts, Old Japanese and ''Kanbun'' texts. Even for the native Japanese speakers, the above texts are difficult to read and understand. But the stream of texts is maybe correct. :01 is the Posthumous name of Niigasa (mother of Emperor Kanmu), {{lang|ja|天高知日之子姫尊 (あめ・たかしる・ひのこ・ひめ・の・みこと, Ame-takashiru-hinoko-hime-no-mikoto)}}. 09 is the reason why this Posthumous name was selected. The portion F of [[User:Asieon]] is as follows: :'''''The queen mother, therefore I, am the direct descendant of them.''''' In the original Texts, there are no words or sentences corresponding to this F statement. This is not mistranslation. But intentional false translation, or fantasy, propaganda, or other non-good faith edit. Who is "I" in this false translation? "False-2" says "Author: Emperor Kanmu". It means this "I" is Emperor Kanmu. Then the above statement is "''The queen mother, therefore I, Emperor Kanmu, am the direct descendant of them.''" But there is no such sentence in the original texts. The original texts say: ::{{lang|ja| 04 后先出自百濟武寧王之子純陀太子。}} ::04] Empress is descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). This is clearly written in the ''Shoku Nihongi''. But this is not considered as historical fact. The Japanese standard Encyclopedia cited in Kotobank do not show this as a historical fact. Father of Niigasa (grandfather of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|和乙継}} (Yamato no Ototsugu). Kotobank says: : {{lang|ja|和乙継 (?－?) 奈良時代の官吏。祖先は百済(くだら)(朝鮮)の人。}} : Yamato no Ototsugu (? - ?) An official in the Nara period. His ancestor is people of Kudara (Baekje), Chōsen (korea). [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%92%8C%E4%B9%99%E7%B6%99-1118051 Ototsugu in Kotobank]. :But no mention that he is descendant of the royal family of Baekje. Mother of Niigasa (grandmother of emperor Kanmu) is {{lang|ja|土師真妹}} (Haji no Maimo). There is no page of Haji no Maimo in Kotobank. The representative encyclopedia of Japan do not say that Niigasa is a descendant of the royal family of Baekju. But the statement in the ''Shoku Nihongi'' clearly writes Niigasa is a descendant of Crown prince Junda, son of King Bunei of Kudara (Baekju). Empress Dowager is descendant of him. :Again, King Tobo (都慕王) is [[Dongmyeong of Goguryeo]], Kahaku (河伯) is [[Habaek]], the daughter of Kahaku is [[Lady Yuhwa]] also known as "Habaeknyeo (河伯女)". It seems to me that your argument revolves around [[Literal translation|literal translations]] of the passages found in the Shoku Nihongi, where for convenience sake, I believe the overall meaning behind the statement is more important here. :Kammu is clearly stating or emphasizing his holy connections to his ancestors to honor his late mother '''AND''' her clan. Otherwise, there would be no reason to add this piece of information alluding to people who have nothing to do with the Japanese Imperial line. Not only does he encompass his immediate Japanese lineage, he also stretches into the kings of ancient [[Baekje]], the founder, and even the King of [[Goguryeo]], his mother from [[Buyeo]] and a river god. :[[Emperor Kanmu]] (Japan) → [[Takano no Niigasa]] (Japan) → [[Prince Junda]] (Japan) → [[Muryeong of Baekje]] (Baekje) → [[Onjo of Baekje]] (Baekje) → [[Dongmyeong of Goguryeo]] (Goguryeo) → [[Lady Yuhwa]] (Buyeo) → [[Habaek]] (God) :Again, I will concede the point that the translation isn't perfect, but that can be fixed. The only reason why the information was there in the first place was to indicate Kanmu's show of interest in his foreign bloodline. That fact is evident in his foreign/domestic policies revolving around other clans of Baekje origins PLUS his marriage to other Korea-originating clan women. This was just to preface why he was so open-minded.
